epics.title: Domain (COMPLETADA ✅)
epics.summary: Domain models and enums for tasks and users.

stories.title: As a developer, I want a TaskItem domain model with Title, Description, Status, Priority, Creator, and AssignedTo fields, so that the business entity is properly represented in code.
acceptance_criteria: Given the domain layer, When I inspect TaskItem, Then it has Title, Description, Status, Priority, Creator, and AssignedTo fields.

stories.title: As a developer, I want TaskItemStatus and TaskItemPriority enums, so that status and priority values are type-safe and constrained.
acceptance_criteria: Given the domain layer, When I inspect enums, Then TaskItemStatus and TaskItemPriority exist with appropriate values.

stories.title: As a developer, I want a User domain model with unique email, so that task ownership and assignment are trackable.
acceptance_criteria: Given the domain layer, When I inspect User, Then it has Id, Name, Email, PasswordHash, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt fields.

epics.title: Application Layer — CQRS (COMPLETADA ✅)
epics.summary: CQRS commands, queries, handlers, validators, and mappings.

stories.title: As a developer, I want a CreateTaskCommand and handler, so that task creation logic is encapsulated and testable in isolation.
acceptance_criteria: Given a CreateTaskCommand, When the handler runs, Then a new task is created.

stories.title: As a developer, I want an UpdateTaskCommand and handler with partial-update semantics, so that only the provided fields are modified on the task.
acceptance_criteria: Given an UpdateTaskCommand with partial fields, When the handler runs, Then only the provided fields are updated.

stories.title: As a developer, I want a DeleteTaskCommand and handler, so that tasks can be removed by id.
acceptance_criteria: Given a DeleteTaskCommand, When the handler runs, Then the task is deleted.

stories.title: As a developer, I want a GetTaskByIdQuery and handler, so that a single task can be retrieved with full creator and assignee details.
acceptance_criteria: Given a GetTaskByIdQuery, When the handler runs, Then the task is returned with creator and assignee details.

stories.title: As a developer, I want a ListTasksQuery and handler that supports filtering by Status, Priority, and AssignedToId, sorting by Title, Priority, or CreatedAt, and 1-based pagination with configurable page size (default 7), so that consumers can browse and filter the task list efficiently.
acceptance_criteria: Given a ListTasksQuery with filters, When the handler runs, Then filtered, sorted, paginated tasks are returned.
tasks: Implement ListTasksQueryHandler with filtering, sorting, pagination

stories.title: As a developer, I want ITaskRepository and IUserRepository interfaces, so that the application layer is decoupled from the persistence implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Given the application layer, When I inspect interfaces, Then ITaskRepository and IUserRepository exist.

stories.title: As a developer, I want FluentValidation validators for CreateTaskCommand and UpdateTaskCommand, so that constraint violations surface with clear error messages before handler execution.
acceptance_criteria: Given invalid input, When the validator runs, Then validation errors are returned.

stories.title: As a developer, I want AutoMapper mappings from TaskItem to TaskResult, so that domain models are safely projected to application DTOs without leaking persistence internals.
acceptance_criteria: Given a TaskItem, When mapped, Then a TaskResult is returned with correct fields.

stories.title: As a developer, I want a PostgreSQL-backed AppDbContext with EF Core, so that tasks and users are persisted in a relational store.
acceptance_criteria: Given the infrastructure layer, When I inspect AppDbContext, Then it connects to PostgreSQL.

stories.title: As a developer, I want a TaskRepository implementing ITaskRepository with GetByIdAsync, GetAllAsync, AddAsync, UpdateAsync, and DeleteAsync, so that all CRUD operations are available to the application layer.
acceptance_criteria: Given a TaskRepository, When I call CRUD methods, Then tasks are persisted correctly.
tasks: Implement TaskRepository with all CRUD methods

stories.title: As a developer, I want an EF Core migration (Init) that creates the Tasks and Users tables, so that the schema is version-controlled and reproducible.
acceptance_criteria: Given the migration, When applied, Then Tasks and Users tables exist.

stories.title: As a developer, I want Status and Priority stored as strings in the database, so that enum values are human-readable in the DB and resilient to reordering.
acceptance_criteria: Given the schema, When I inspect Status and Priority columns, Then they are stored as strings.
tasks: Configure enum-to-string conversion in EF Core

epics.title: HTTP Contracts (COMPLETADA ✅)
epics.summary: Request and response DTOs for HTTP API.

stories.title: As a developer, I want CreateTaskRequest, UpdateTaskRequest, and TaskListRequest DTOs, so that HTTP input shapes are explicitly defined and decoupled from domain commands.
acceptance_criteria: Given the contracts layer, When I inspect request DTOs, Then CreateTaskRequest, UpdateTaskRequest, and TaskListRequest exist.

stories.title: As a developer, I want TaskResponse and TaskListResponse DTOs, so that HTTP output shapes are stable and versioned independently of the domain model.
acceptance_criteria: Given the contracts layer, When I inspect response DTOs, Then TaskResponse and TaskListResponse exist.

stories.title: As a developer, I want all API responses wrapped in { "ok": true, "data": ... } on success and { "ok": false, "error": { "code": "...", "message": "..." } } on failure, so that clients have a uniform response contract regardless of the operation.
acceptance_criteria: Given an API response, When I inspect the envelope, Then it has ok and data/error fields.
tasks: Create ApiResult helper with envelope format

epics.title: API — Carter Endpoint Modules (COMPLETADA ✅)
epics.summary: Carter modules exposing task CRUD endpoints.

stories.title: As an API consumer, I want GET /tasks with optional ?status=, ?priority=, ?assignedToId=, ?sortBy=, ?sortDesc=, ?page=, and ?size= query params, so that I can retrieve a filtered, sorted, paginated list of tasks directly from a browser address bar.
acceptance_criteria: Given the /tasks endpoint, When I call it with query params, Then filtered, sorted, paginated tasks are returned.

stories.title: As an API consumer, I want GET /tasks/{id}, so that I can retrieve the full detail of a single task including creator name and assignee name.
acceptance_criteria: Given the /tasks/{id} endpoint, When I call it, Then the task detail is returned.

stories.title: As an API consumer, I want GET /tasks/create?title=&description=&priority=&assignedToId=&creatorId=, so that I can create a new task by navigating to a URL or pasting it in a browser for easy demoing.
acceptance_criteria: Given the /tasks/create endpoint, When I call it with query params, Then a new task is created.

stories.title: As an API consumer, I want GET /tasks/{id}/update?title=&description=&status=&priority=&assignedToId=, so that I can partially update a task with only the fields I pass.
acceptance_criteria: Given the /tasks/{id}/update endpoint, When I call it with query params, Then the task is partially updated.

stories.title: As an API consumer, I want GET /tasks/{id}/delete, so that I can delete a task by navigating to a single URL.
acceptance_criteria: Given the /tasks/{id}/delete endpoint, When I call it, Then the task is deleted.

stories.title: As an API consumer, I want all request and response bodies (including Authorization headers and credentials) logged at info level, so that every interaction is fully reproducible from logs.
acceptance_criteria: Given an API call, When it is made, Then the request and response are logged at info level.
tasks: Implement logging middleware for request/response bodies

stories.title: As a developer, I want Carter ICarterModule implementations registered via app.MapCarter(), so that routes are organized by resource without manual route registration boilerplate.
acceptance_criteria: Given the API, When it starts, Then Carter modules are registered and routes are available.

epics.title: Docker Compose — Portabilidad
epics.summary: Containerize the API + PostgreSQL stack for one-command startup.

stories.title: As a developer, I want a docker-compose.yml that starts the API and PostgreSQL with a single command, so the entire stack runs without local dependency installation.
acceptance_criteria: Given docker-compose.yml exists, When I run docker compose up, Then both api and db containers start and the API is reachable on port 5255.
tasks: Write docker-compose.yml with api and db services
Test docker compose up from clean checkout

stories.title: As a developer, I want the API container to run EF Core migrations on startup, so a fresh environment is schema-ready without manual steps.
acceptance_criteria: Given a fresh PostgreSQL container, When the API container starts, Then migrations are applied and the Tasks/Users tables exist.
tasks: Add migration runner to Program.cs startup
Verify migrations run on first boot

stories.title: As a developer, I want environment variables for connection strings and secrets managed via Docker Compose, so the app is configurable without changing code.
acceptance_criteria: Given docker-compose.yml, When I set environment variables, Then the API picks them up without code changes.
tasks: Document required env vars in README

epics.title: Frontend — UI Responsive
epics.summary: Build a responsive, mobile-first task management UI.

stories.title: As a user, I want a task list view that shows all tasks with their title, status, priority, and assignee, so I have an at-a-glance overview of the project.
acceptance_criteria: Given tasks exist, When I load the list view, Then each task shows title, status, priority, and assignee.
tasks: Render task cards with key fields

stories.title: As a user, I want to filter the task list by status and priority from the UI, so I can focus on the tasks that are relevant to me.
acceptance_criteria: Given the task list, When I apply a status filter, Then only matching tasks are shown.
tasks: Add filter dropdowns for status and priority
Wire query params to API call

stories.title: As a user, I want to sort the task list by title, priority, or creation date, so I can organize my view to match my workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Given the task list, When I click a sort header, Then tasks are reordered accordingly.
tasks: Wire sortBy and sortDesc query params

stories.title: As a user, I want pagination controls in the task list (previous/next, page indicator), so I can navigate large datasets without overwhelming the view.
acceptance_criteria: Given a paginated task list, When I click next, Then the next page of tasks is loaded.
tasks: Wire page and size query params

stories.title: As a user, I want a task detail view showing all fields of a task, so I can see the full context of a work item.
acceptance_criteria: Given a task in the list, When I click it, Then a detail view shows all fields.

stories.title: As a user, I want a form to create a new task with fields for title, description, priority, and assignee, so I can add work items to the system.
acceptance_criteria: Given the create form, When I fill it and submit, Then a new task is created and appears in the list.
tasks: Add fields for title, description, priority, assignee

stories.title: As a user, I want to edit an existing task's title, description, status, priority, and assignee from the UI, so I can keep task information up to date.
acceptance_criteria: Given a task in edit mode, When I change fields and save, Then the task is updated.

stories.title: As a user, I want a delete confirmation and one-click delete for a task, so I can remove completed or invalid items.
acceptance_criteria: Given a task, When I click delete, Then a confirmation appears and the task is removed on confirm.
tasks: Add delete button to task detail

stories.title: As a user, I want the UI to be fully responsive and usable on mobile, tablet, and desktop, so I can manage tasks from any device.
acceptance_criteria: Given the UI, When I resize the browser, Then the layout adapts gracefully.

epics.title: Authentication
epics.summary: Implement JWT-based authentication for secure task management.

stories.title: As a developer, I want a UserRepository implementing IUserRepository, so user lookup and creation are available to auth handlers.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user email, When I call GetByEmailAsync, Then the user is returned if they exist.

stories.title: As a developer, I want a RegisterCommand and handler that creates a new User with a hashed password, so new users can be onboarded.
acceptance_criteria: Given a registration request, When the handler runs, Then a user is created with a hashed password.

stories.title: As a developer, I want a LoginQuery and handler that validates credentials and returns a JWT, so authenticated sessions can be established.
acceptance_criteria: Given valid credentials, When the handler runs, Then a JWT is returned.

stories.title: As a developer, I want an IJwtTokenGenerator implementation that signs tokens with a secret and expiration, so token generation is centralized and configurable.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user, When I call GenerateToken, Then a signed JWT is returned.
tasks: Configure secret and expiration from appsettings

stories.title: As a developer, I want an IPasswordHasher implementation, so passwords are never stored in plain text.
acceptance_criteria: Given a password, When I hash it, Then a secure hash is returned.

stories.title: As an API consumer, I want GET /auth/register?email=&password=&name=, so new accounts can be created from a browser.
acceptance_criteria: Given the register endpoint, When I call it with valid params, Then a user is created.

stories.title: As an API consumer, I want GET /auth/login?email=&password= returning { ok: true, data: { token: ... } }, so a session token can be obtained from a browser.
acceptance_criteria: Given the login endpoint, When I call it with valid credentials, Then a JWT is returned.

stories.title: As an API consumer, I want unauthorized requests to task endpoints to return { ok: false, error: { code: Auth.Unauthorized, message: ... } }, so protected resources are inaccessible without a valid token.
acceptance_criteria: Given a protected endpoint, When I call it without a token, Then a 401 error is returned.
tasks: Return error envelope on auth failure

stories.title: As a user, I want a login page with email and password fields, so I can authenticate and access the task management UI.
acceptance_criteria: Given the login page, When I enter credentials and submit, Then I am logged in.
tasks: Redirect to task list on success

stories.title: As a user, I want a registration page with name, email, and password fields, so I can create a new account.
acceptance_criteria: Given the registration page, When I fill it and submit, Then an account is created.

stories.title: As a user, I want to be redirected to the task list after a successful login, so I land in a productive state immediately.
acceptance_criteria: Given a successful login, When the response is received, Then I am redirected to the task list.
tasks: Add redirect logic after login success

stories.title: As a user, I want my session to persist across browser refreshes (JWT stored client-side), so I do not need to re-authenticate on every visit.
acceptance_criteria: Given a logged-in session, When I refresh the page, Then I remain logged in.
tasks: Attach JWT to all API requests

stories.title: As a user, I want a logout button that clears my session, so I can safely end my session on a shared device.
acceptance_criteria: Given a logged-in session, When I click logout, Then the JWT is cleared and I am redirected to login.
